title: From<TssError> funnels TPM-layer error into Transport variant
description: wolftpm-tss/src/error.rs implements From<TssError> for Error by mapping to Error::Transport { code: e.get() as i32 }. TssError is a TPM command-layer result code (e.g. TPM_RC_FAILURE from the TPM itself), not a transport failure. Stuffing it into the Transport variant means callers cannot distinguish 'the socket dropped' from 'the TPM returned an error code' — both look like Error::Transport. This also silently casts u32 to i32 without overflow check. Idiomatic Rust would add a dedicated Error::Tpm { rc: u32 } variant (or Error::TssError(TssError)) so callers can match on the class of failure. The From impl is required by tpm2-rs-client's Connection trait bound, but the mapping choice is wrong.
